items.md: and when a slide of Donald is projected toward Scrooge by the ray, Scrooge will be overwhelmed by the urge to be generous to Donald. But Donald unwittingly shows Scrooge a slide of a dog, and Scrooge buys the dog all of the presents listed in the song "The Twelve Days of Christmas." *(Aug. 14, 1963)*

items.md: ## 48 - March 1964 - 36 pages
items.value: 48 - March 1964 - 36 pages

items.md: Front cover: As Magica de Spell grins in the background, Scrooge is astonished to see Donald's and the nephews' faces reflected as Magica's in a pond. (Illustrating "The Many Faces of Magica de Spell.") *(October 15, 1963)*
items.value: Front cover: As Magica de Spell grins in the background, Scrooge is astonished to see Donald's and the nephews' faces reflected as Magica's in a pond. (Illustrating "The Many Faces of Magica de Spell.") (October 15, 1963)

items.md: Reprinted: *Uncle Scrooge* No. 138, March 1977.
items.value: Reprinted: Uncle Scrooge No. 138, March 1977.

items.md: UNCLE SCROOGE The Many Faces of Magica de Spell - 22 - Magica invents a potion that, when sprayed on a person's face, makes that face look like the face at which the person last looked. Learning of the potion, Scrooge flees from Duckburg with his Old Number One Dime and hides in an unknown jungle valley that is populated by a strange race of faceless people. Magica follows him there and sprays his face. *(October 5, 1963)*
items.value: UNCLE SCROOGE The Many Faces of Magica de Spell - 22 - Magica invents a potion that, when sprayed on a person's face, makes that face look like the face at which the person last looked. Magica follows him there and sprays his face. (October 5, 1963)

items.md: GYRO GEARLOOSE Jonah Gyro - 4 - Gyro's use of a little mechanical fish to lure salmon irks some other fishermen, but when he tosses it away, it lures a school of salmon and a whale toward the shore. *(Sept. 16, 1963)*

items.md: UNCLE SCROOGE The Loony Lunar Gold Rush - 17 - Scrooge is lecturing the Junior Woodchucks on the follies of gold fever when the first astronauts return from the moon with the news that gold is thick there. The ducks build their own small rocket and go to the moon, where Scrooge sets up the rocket as a store selling everything at gold rush prices. A villain named Dan McShrew lures the
items.value: UNCLE SCROOGE The Loony Lunar Gold Rush - 17 - Scrooge is lecturing the Junior Woodchucks on the follies of gold fever when the first astronauts return from the moon with the news that gold is thick there. The ducks build their own small rocket and go to the moon, where Scrooge sets up the rocket as a store selling everything at gold rush prices. A villain named Dan McShrew lures the

items.md: ducks away from the rocket and leaves them to die, leading to a parody of Service's "The Shooting of Dan McGrew." *(Nov. 12, 1963)*

items.md: ## 50 - July 1964 - 36 pages
items.value: 50 - July 1964 - 36 pages

items.md: Front cover: The ducks are riding on a flying carpet, which is being unraveled by a roc. (Illustrating "Rug Riders in the Sky.") *(Dec. 26, 1963)*
items.value: Front cover: The ducks are riding on a flying carpet, which is being unraveled by a roc. (Illustrating "Rug Riders in the Sky.") (Dec. 26, 1963)

items.md: A Barks sketch for the cover of this issue — rejected by Western — shows Scrooge being tossed by a magic carpet while the other ducks flee. The title of the story, as given on the drawing, was to have been "Rug Bugs in Old Bugdud," and not "Rug Riders in the Sky."
items.value: A Barks sketch for the cover of this issue — rejected by Western — shows Scrooge being tossed by a magic carpet while the other ducks flee. The title of the story, as given on the drawing, was to have been "Rug Bugs in Old Bugdud," and not "Rug Riders in the Sky."

items.md: Reprinted: *Uncle Scrooge* No. 116, December 1974.
items.value: Reprinted: Uncle Scrooge No. 116, December 1974.

items.md: UNCLE SCROOGE Rug Riders in the Sky - 16 - Magica de Spell, in a bazaar in Baghdad, finds an authentic flying carpet, and tricks Scrooge into buying it. Magica steals the carpet twice, but each time the ducks regain it, and the carpet finally carries them to a cave containing the treasure of Aladdin. *(Dec. 26, 1963)*
items.value: UNCLE SCROOGE Rug Riders in the Sky - 16 - Magica de Spell, in a bazaar in Baghdad, finds an authentic flying carpet, and tricks Scrooge into buying it. Magica steals the carpet twice, but each time the ducks regain it, and the carpet finally carries them to a cave containing the treasure of Aladdin. (Dec. 26, 1963)

items.md: ## 51 - August 1964 - 36 pages
items.value: 51 - August 1964 - 36 pages

items.md: Front cover: A worried-looking Scrooge runs while holding aloft a crate that contains a mixture of lettuce and currency. (Illustrating "How Green Was My Lettuce.") *(Jan. 18, 1964)*
items.value: Front cover: A worried-looking Scrooge runs while holding aloft a crate that contains a mixture of lettuce and currency. (Illustrating "How Green Was My Lettuce.") (Jan. 18, 1964)

items.md: Reprinted: *Uncle Scrooge* No. 95, October 1971.
items.value: Reprinted: Uncle Scrooge No. 95, October 1971.

items.md: UNCLE SCROOGE How Green Was My Lettuce - 15 - Gophers undermine the foundations of Scrooge's money bin, and he has the coins exchanged for bills to reduce the bin's weight. The gophers belong to the Beagle Boys, who are planning to slide the bin down a tunnel and into a pit on their property. When the plot is discovered, Scrooge and Donald disguise themselves as lettuce farmers and drive out of town in a truck filled with lettuce that conceals Scrooge's paper money. *(Jan. 18, 1964)*
items.value: UNCLE SCROOGE How Green Was My Lettuce - 15 - Gophers undermine the foundations of Scrooge's money bin, and he has the coins exchanged for bills to reduce the bin's weight. The gophers belong to the Beagle Boys, who are planning to slide the bin down a tunnel and into a pit on their property. When the plot is discovered, Scrooge and Donald disguise themselves as lettuce farmers and drive out of town in a truck filled with lettuce that conceals Scrooge's paper money. (Jan. 18, 1964)
